title: API v2 tests: catch up to moving target
body:
Lots has changed since I checked this in:

 * Switch to new podman system service invocation
 * Get rid of stderr special-casing
 * Cleaner port-open testing
 * t() no longer needs leading '.'; this allows jq
   features such as 'length' and perhaps other filters
 * /containers API now needs ?all=true; and container
   state is now 6 instead of 4
 * special-case handling of 204 - assert no output

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@baude @jwhonce PTAL; FYI tests are still failing because:
```
not ok 26 GET libpod/containers/json?a=b : status
#  expected: 400
#    actual: 200
...
not ok 68 GET libpod/pods/foo/exists : status
#  expected: 204
#    actual: 200
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

```
+0204s] panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference [recovered]
[+0204s] 	panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[+0204s] [signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x230 pc=0x109437c]
```
This test failure is unlikely to be caused by my code...

/hold

I'd like to get #5169 in, then update. (Need to update anyway, because in true moving-target fashion the latest `master` completely changed `/containers`)
With this, all the apiv2 tests are passing. Whew. (And no, I'm not enabling them in CI yet).
